reviews.author: Ric K
reviews.text: Truly a must see. A bit of a stair climber to get there, but well worth it. 1.4 km of amazing caves in a huge and high setting. You can do another 4km extra if you want to go further with a hired guide.

reviews.author: Charbeel 84
reviews.text: I put 4 stars instead of 5 because of the entrance fee.. You must pay for the Buggy that'll bring you to the beggining of the stairs. I asked not to pay for it cause I wantws to walk and I say there was a walking path as well but the lady didn't want to heat nothing from me and told me I must pay for the transportation.. Apart from that, The Cave itself is just stunning, very huge and so beautiful. So many different stalagtites and stalagmites and pretty massive. Even if there is a walking path we'll maintained, the cave feels still natural, I was surprised about that. I visited around 9am and I was almost alone, about 10/10h30 some more people were already coming from the bus tour and the cave was already more noisy. If you're going by yourself and want to avoid the crowd, early in the morning is nice or during lunch time as well.

reviews.author: Gaurav Rane
reviews.text: Paradise Cave in Vietnam is a true natural masterpiece that lives up to its name. Located in Phong Nha–Ke Bang National Park, the cave is one of the longest dry caves in Asia and is renowned for its breathtaking scale and beauty. From the moment you step inside, you are greeted by a vast underground world filled with towering stalactites and stalagmites, formed over millions of years. The interior feels almost cathedral-like, with high ceilings and grand chambers that create a sense of awe and wonder. Soft lighting highlights the intricate rock formations, allowing visitors to appreciate their unique shapes and textures without disturbing the cave’s natural atmosphere. A well-maintained wooden walkway makes exploration easy while preserving the fragile environment. What sets Paradise Cave apart is its quiet, almost sacred ambiance. The cool air, echoing silence, and majestic formations create a peaceful and unforgettable experience. Visiting Paradise Cave is not just about sightseeing—it’s about witnessing the incredible power of nature and leaving with a deep appreciation for the hidden beauty beneath Vietnam’s landscape.

reviews.author: Igor Koller
reviews.text: Astonishing cave! Huge halls and beautiful formations. Very easy access. If you can, buy the ticket with the electric car to get there from the parking lot. It saves you a 2km walk and costs just 15k vnd more. I arrived the cave 9am on a tuesday and there was nobody there yet. Two hours later, lots of buses packed with tourist arrived, so try to be early. The boardwalk makes it super easy and safe to go to the end. A must see when in Phong Nha!

reviews.author: Linh Nguyễn
reviews.text: Paradise Cave is truly magical and so beautiful!!! The guide we met inside made our trip even better. He was super friendly and helped our family take so many beautiful photos 🥹. We really appreciated his kindness. Such a great experience!

reviews.author: Shekhar “Shekhoooo”
reviews.text: The guided tour may feel a bit expensive, but it is absolutely worth it. The tour includes a dedicated guide along with support staff who carry lunch and serve it inside the cave. The caves are truly enjoyable, and the staff are very kind and helpful. Since we visited during the winter season, we were able to go only up to 4 km inside the cave. In summer, however, visitors can explore up to 7 km. Swimming is allowed at the 4 km point, where the water is clean and beautifully green. Visiting during the summer season is highly recommended, as you can enjoy kayaking, swimming, and trekking deeper into the cave.

reviews.author: Alexis S
reviews.text: This was spectacular. It is a 10/10. I was in complete awe in this cave. It is a fun but moderately difficult walk up to the cave. Take the switch backs, the stairs are a slog. The cave itself was so much larger than I expected. Pictures cannot capture how amazing it was. There are quite a few stairs going in and how of the cave to the boardwalk so a bit of fitness is required. I am so happy that I got to see this, it is more spectacular than the other caves we visited in the area. I strongly recommend visiting this cave! The best time is lunchtime, between the morning and afternoon rush.

reviews.author: Aidan & Marybeth
reviews.text: We did this as apart of a full day tour, we would really recommend paradise cave if you are in the area. They provide a buggy service to the base of the hill. There is then a sloped 10-20 minute walk to the entrance of the cave (toilets near the top with clear signs). At the entrance there is a place to sit with fans if needed and you can buy cold drinks. The cave itself is very impressive compared to other caves that we have seen, the inside is massive!

reviews.author: Lauren May
reviews.text: The caves were extraordinary. Good 20-30 minutes to walk up if you wish but also can get a shuttle bus. My only niggle is that all of the staff in the cave were on their phones playing videos which disturbed the peacefulness of the cave. Definitely worth the price (175k VND per person) for the beauty of it.

reviews.author: Andy Web
reviews.text: Spectacular! Little buggy car ride up to the path to start the walk to the cave entrance. Once inside the views are amazing, and the different range of colours - wow. The temperature is comfortable but does get a little warmer the further you go in.

reviews.author: Donna Murphy
reviews.text: We visited on a very rainy day but in the end it was probably an advantage as there weren’t many people there. The walk up to the entrance is not for everyone but we took our time and made it. Inside the cave is spectacular with some of the best stalactites and stalagmites I’ve seen. The cavern is massive and I recommend walking all the way to the end. The most impressive thing is that there was phone signal in there! Amazing.

reviews.author: Hannah Lee
reviews.text: The cave is huge and definitely worth checking out! We walked a full kilometer into the cave and were stunned at the size and variety of formations. The walk up was a little tough but doable, and there are cold drinks at the top! To my fellow tourists, please remember to be respectful and speak quietly in the cave to preserve it!!

reviews.author: Bram Delmot
reviews.text: It was the most impressive cave I´ve seen so far. Really huge, nice easy walk. Not too cold in there, nice lighting, etc. A bit of a hike to get there, you need to walk up for 600m but it´s worth it! Our guide was nice too, good level of English!

reviews.author: Vera Williams
reviews.text: Worth the trip and the money. 150k entry and 5k motorbike parking. Walk 20mins to the start of the proper walk to the cave, or take the buggy. From cave walk start it’s about 15-20mins walk uphill to the cave entrance. Then 30-40 mins of walking in the cave (walk in about 1km turn around and walk back). Cave is amazing. Huge in size and incredible structures and features. Highly recommend for proper wow factor. Do half day at botanical gardens and half day here and that’s an epic day. Approx 2hrs total time to spend there. Plenty of food and drink stores at the bottom car park.

reviews.author: Shilpa hegde
reviews.text: It's beautiful earth's secret one can see.. huge cave of 31km with 1 km accessible without guide and is well maintained with walkways. Beautiful colours which almost makes you believe that you are in another world..

reviews.author: Kevin G
reviews.text: Great cave with awesome high cathedral like ceilings, some great stalagmites. Were the names are given, particularly look out for the “jade rabbit” Be aware it’s a long walk uphill to the cave entrance and a very long walk in the cave itself with lots of steps, so only for the reasonably fit.

reviews.author: Jonathan Weaver
reviews.text: 570 meters to entrance - not a stressful climb 1km of deck inside = 2 km walk inside Worth the visit

reviews.author: Sneh K
reviews.text: A true paradise underneath. It's a huge cave to explore, 250k entry fees makes sense for the kind of maintenance needed. One star less for forcing to buy buggy ticket, it's barely 1.5km walk.

reviews.author: Bar Co
reviews.text: I been in quite a few caves and this was the most impressive one the entrance was around 275000 better to come around 12-1500 so u have time just to hung out and feel the special atmosphere

reviews.author: Floww
reviews.text: Breathtaking views, and a long trek to the end of the cave. Bring a good, focusable flashlight to see all the water streams. Went on a random Wednesday, the cave experience seems betters with less people. Bought tickets at gate with no problem.

reviews.author: H K
reviews.text: This cavern is huge and extremely beautiful. This cave is the longest in Asia spanning 31 Km in range, but for everyone to sight see only 1 Km opened. You can book a tour in advance that will take you to 2 caverns a day. The cave is very big, you can have a look around inside and enjoy the view. There's an expedition for 7 Km part of the cave, it's a one day tracking expedition. If you love cave exploration taking the 7 Km tour is going to be fun.

reviews.author: Rick Jardon
reviews.text: Very large and impressive cave. You descend into the cave by a long staircase down. Once at the bottom, there are nice large walkways that allow use to marvel at the vastness of the cave. Essentially, you walk for about 30 mins and then walk back and ascend the same stairs. Definitely worth seeing. In case you can only go to one of the 3 featured caves (Paradise, Dark and Phong Nha), choose Phong Nha. The cave is not as cavernous but it’s just as impressive and the real highlight is the boat ride in that lasts a good 30 mins.

reviews.author: Lenna
reviews.text: We loved exploring paradise cave! It is a bit of a hike to get up there so wear good shoes. It can also get very slippery inside, we saw someone fall down so use the handrails and watch your step. It was a really great experience overall.

reviews.author: Virginia Lio
reviews.text: 🪨 Paradise Cave – Stunning & Peaceful Visited with Sovaba Tours from Hue, and it was absolutely worth the long trip! Our guide Victor was amazing — local, friendly, and very knowledgeable about the area and the caves. He took great care of the group. Paradise Cave is huge, quiet, and beautifully lit. The limestone formations are incredible — walking inside feels like entering a natural cathedral. Peaceful and awe-inspiring. Highly recommend visiting, and if you can, go with Sovaba and ask for Victor!

reviews.author: Sandrine Demers-Telmosse
reviews.text: Highly recommend - very impressive. Don’t go with a tour, rent a scooter and go :)

reviews.author: tahra brady
reviews.text: So glad we visited. The most beautiful cave we have ever seen! So much to look at. We were lucky as it wasn't very busy either. Bit of a walk up to the cave but well worth it

reviews.author: Nikolaus Mairböck
reviews.text: Huge cave you can visit. The hike up is a bit steep but not too long, the walk in the cave is about 1km on wooden bridges

reviews.author: Vy T. Zunker
reviews.text: There are better places. Paradise Cave itself is breathtaking—a true natural wonder that deserves respect and preservation. Sadly, the overall experience is undermined by how it’s managed. Tickets are quite expensive for what you get. After purchasing, you can take an electric golf cart to the trailhead, then hike about 500m uphill to reach the cave entrance. While the cave is worth the effort, the way it’s operated takes away from the experience. We didn’t join any tour, but it was clear that the company running the cave prioritizes quantity over quality. They schedule too many tours at the same time, causing congestion and chaos inside the cave. To make matters worse, many visitors block the narrow walkways to take endless photos and group pictures, which only adds to the crowding and frustration. Instead of the peaceful, awe-inspiring experience you’d expect, it often feels stressful and overwhelming. The atmosphere is loud, unorganized, and lacks any real crowd control. It’s disappointing to see such an extraordinary place treated more as a money-making venture than a site to be protected and appreciated. Paradise Cave is stunning and absolutely worth seeing—but be prepared for high ticket prices, overcrowding, and inconsiderate behavior that take away from its natural beauty.

reviews.author: Liam Doherty
reviews.text: Overall, this is a great walk and a decent cave. It’s long and interesting, with some impressive features like large stalagmites and vast open chambers. Disclaimer: I visited without a guide and went on my own. The walk is easy and not physically demanding. Allow around 2 hours if you’re taking your time, including the golf cart ride and the walk up to the cave. There’s a long, well-maintained walkway that’s easy to navigate, and it wasn’t too crowded when I visited. Personally, I prefer more adventurous caves with climbing, no railings, and minimal lighting, so this wasn’t quite my style. For me it’s a 6/10 overall. However, if you’re looking for an easy, accessible, and scenic cave experience, I’d rate it closer to an 8/10.

reviews.author: Loz Dale
reviews.text: If you come to Vietnam come and visit this cave, it's amazing as your boat ride into the cave is probably a one off experience.
